description_long: This is a vintage single-engine monoplane with a streamlined fuselage and a fixed, taildragger landing gear configuration. The wings are straight and mounted low on the fuselage, with prominent black markings reading 'D 366' on both wings. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the cockpit is situated just behind the engine, featuring a clear canopy that appears to be enclosed. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted in a matching light color. The overall finish is smooth and metallic, with subtle blue and gray tones accenting the body.
description: A vintage, single-engine low-wing monoplane with a streamlined fuselage, fixed taildragger gear, and enclosed cockpit. The airplane features straight wings with bold markings and a smooth metallic finish.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the propeller, nose, and cockpit canopy, with the left wing and landing gear clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail section, vertical stabilizer, and the left wing's full span with visible markings.
description_view_2: Rear-right view focusing on the tail assembly, right wing, and the rear fuselage contours.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the propeller, cockpit, and right wing, with the landing gear and nose details prominent.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy just behind the engine.
